articles.title: Op Checkmate: 30 Indians held for illegally working as truck drivers in US, deportation likely
articles.description: US Border Patrol's Operation Checkmate has led to the arrest of approximately 30 Indian nationals working illegally as commercial truck drivers in Arizona. These individuals, along with others from different countries, are facing deportation. The operation aimed to enhance public safety by enforcing immigration laws and identifying undocumented drivers operating heavy vehicles.

articles.description: In the cases of both South Korea and Taiwan, the stock market rally is fuelled by a powerful surge in semiconductor giants that are benefiting from the global artificial intelligence boom.

articles.title: TOI's IPL team of the season: Sooryavanshi-Kohli at the top; Patidar named captain
articles.description: In the sensational IPL-2026 season, young talent Vaibhav Sooryavanshi took the spotlight, smashing records left and right. Not to be outdone, the legendary Virat Kohli proved that age is just a number with his phenomenal performances on the field. Meanwhile, Rajat Patidar steered RCB to triumph, securing a remarkable second title.
articles.full_text: Hindol Basu is a Principal Correspondent with the The Times of India. Over the years, as a sports journalist, Hindol has covered important events like the 2012 London Olympics, 2008 Beijing Olympics, 2010 Commonwealth Games and the 2011 Cricket World Cup. Hindol has had a diverse profile having worked in all forms of media - TV, Radio, New Media and Print. Besides, being an avid blogger, Hindol plays the guitar, writes poetry and is interested in photography. Read More

articles.title: Russia launches major attack across Ukraine; several feared trapped after missile-hit building collapses
articles.description: Russia unleashed a major overnight assault on Ukrainian cities, including Kyiv and Dnipro, hours after President Zelenskyy's warning. Fatalities and injuries were reported in Dnipro, with fears of people trapped in Kyiv's rubble. Meanwhile, a Ukrainian drone strike claimed a civilian life in Russia's Kursk region. Peace efforts remain stalled amidst escalating cross-border attacks.

articles.title: An alternative to the Strait of Hormuz? Why Iran is eyeing Yemen's Bab al-Mandab
articles.description: Escalating Middle East tensions are now focusing on the Bab al-Mandab Strait, a crucial global shipping lane. Iran, via its Yemeni allies, may use this chokepoint to pressure adversaries, potentially disrupting vital oil and goods transit. This raises fears of wider conflict impacting international trade routes and energy markets.

articles.title: Bangladesh: Thousands gather to pay final respects to Awami League veteran, despite ban
articles.description: Thousands gathered in Dhaka to honor Awami League veteran Tofail Ahmed, sparking speculation about the party's potential resurgence amidst a ban. This event, coupled with claims of minimal reforms by the interim government and its alignment with certain parties, suggests a possible return for the Awami League. Meanwhile, a minority minister resigned citing health issues.

articles.title: Oil prices today: Crude rises amid uncertainty over US-Iran negotiations and Hormuz reopening
articles.description: Oil prices saw a slight uptick as US-Iran talks and the potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz created market jitters. Conflicting signals from Washington and Tehran fueled uncertainty, with traders closely watching any progress or breakdown in negotiations. Developments around the crucial energy route remain a key focus, impacting global supply routes and risk sentiment.

articles.title: ODI tickets worth Rs 52.36 lakh went unsold: Khanna
articles.description: "Tickets worth Rupees 52,36,000 were unsold. Why all the tickets were not sold-out? DDCA started online ticket sale quite late that too without proper advertisement," Khanna claimed in a media release.

articles.title: CBSE answer book verification portal active, how to apply and complete Aadhaar authentication steps
articles.description: CBSE has activated its portal for Class 12 students to apply for verification of issues in scanned answer books and re-evaluation of answers. The facility is open only to candidates who have received photocopies of evaluated answer sheets and will remain available from June 2, 2026, to June 6, 2026. Aadhaar-based authentication has been made mandatory for login, with provision for using a parent or guardian’s Aadhaar where required, followed by online submission and payment.
articles.full_text: CBSE has activated its portal for Class 12 students to apply for verification of issues in scanned answer books and re-evaluation of answers. The facility is open only to candidates who have received photocopies of evaluated answer sheets and will remain available from June 2, 2026, to June 6, 2026. Aadhaar-based authentication has been made mandatory for login, with provision for using a parent or guardian’s Aadhaar where required, followed by online submission and payment.

articles.title: CBSE opens Class 12 re-evaluation portal after delay, applications accepted till June 6: Direct link
articles.description: CBSE has activated its online portal for students to apply for verification of issues in scanned answer books and for re-evaluation of answers. The facility, available from June 2, 2026, to June 6, 2026, is open only to candidates who have accessed scanned copies of their evaluated answer sheets. Applications must be submitted online with Aadhaar-based login and fee payment. Students can request correction of issues or re-evaluation of selected answers within the prescribed limits.

articles.title: After CBSE OSM row, NTA’s re-examination portal comes under cyber security spotlight as researcher claims data exposure
articles.description: The National Testing Agency’s (NTA) re-examination portal has come under scrutiny after cybersecurity researchers alleged vulnerabilities that could expose administrative data and provide unauthorized access to portal functions. The claims, shared online, remain independently unverified, and NTA has yet to respond to queries seeking clarification. The allegations emerge amid heightened concerns over the security of examination-related digital platforms following the recent controversy surrounding CBSE’s On-Screen Marking (OSM) system.

articles.title: How social media is blurring the line between work and life for Gen Z and turning every post into a professional risk
articles.description: Gen Z employees face a blurred line between personal and professional lives as social media becomes an extension of the workplace. A Zety report reveals 95% avoid posting opinions due to career risks, with 90% experiencing negative consequences. This constant digital visibility forces self-editing and a managed online identity.

articles.title: Taylor Swift announces original song for 'Toy Story 5'
articles.description: Taylor Swift is lending her voice to the highly anticipated 'Toy Story 5' with a new original song, 'I Knew It, I Knew You'. The country-inspired track, inspired by Jessie's past, drops June 5th, ahead of the film's June 19th release. Swift, a lifelong fan, called it a dream come true, hinting at an emotional journey for the beloved cowgirl.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Mark Ruffalo
articles.description: Actor Mark Ruffalo, honored for environmental activism, shared a powerful message at Dickinson College: embracing the unknown fuels personal and artistic growth. He urged stepping outside comfort zones, facing fears like failure, and taking leaps of faith to foster resilience and achieve goals. Ruffalo's insights encourage bold action, emphasizing that growth stems from venturing into uncharted territory.

articles.title: M.I.A. sues Kid Cudi for USD 2.8 million
articles.description: British musician M.I.A. has filed a $2.8 million lawsuit against American rapper Kid Cudi in a California federal court. The legal action follows her abrupt removal from Cudi’s North American Rebel Ragers Tour in May 2026, which she alleges was an unjustified breach of contract and a desperate publicity stunt to boost underperforming ticket sales.
